title: rubus glaucellus var. rigidulatus
intro: Rubus glaucellus var. rigidulatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
